name: Utxesa Reservoir from Aitona — Circular through Segrià
name_original: Embalse de Utxesa desde Aitona — Circular por el Segrià
description: Cycle a moderate 18.6-mile (30.0 km) route around Utxesa Reservoir from Aitona, enjoying wetlands, fruit trees, and diverse birdlife.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Aitona features routes that traverse a landscape shaped by the Segre River, its canals, and the Utxesa Reservoir. The terrain is generally flat to gently rolling, making it suitable for various cycling abilities. This area in Lleida province offers a mix of agricultural views and waterside paths. The region's network of waterways provides distinct features for cycling exploration. Best touring cycling routes around Aitona The most popular touring cycling route is Utxesa Reservoir from Aitona — Circular through Segrià, a 18.6 miles (30.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 6 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a circular journey around the Utxesa Reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Canal de Seròs Sluice Gates – Utxesa Reservoir loop from Aitona, a moderate 23.0 miles (37.0 km) path. This route features both the Canal de Seròs Sluice Gates and the Utxesa Reservoir. Local touring cyclists also love the Utxesa Reservoir – Canal de Seròs Sluice Gates loop from Aitona, a 17.3 miles (27.8 km) trail leading through the Utxesa Reservoir and the Canal de Seròs Sluice Gates, often completed in about 2 hours 2 minutes. Touring cycling around Aitona is defined by its rivers, canals, and reservoirs. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Aitona are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Aitona's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Aitona?
A: There are over 100 touring cycling routes around Aitona, catering to various skill levels. You'll find 8 easy routes, 54 moderate routes, and 41 difficult routes to explore.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Aitona?
A: Yes, many routes in Aitona are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e962204989/utxesa-reservoir-from-aitona-circular-through-segria" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Utxesa Reservoir from Aitona — Circular through Segrià&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate 18.6-mile (30.0 km) journey around the reservoir.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Aitona?
A: The terrain around Aitona is generally flat to gently rolling, shaped by the Segre River, its canals, and the Utxesa Reservoir. This makes it suitable for various cycling abilities, offering a mix of agricultural views and waterside paths.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Aitona?
A: Given the generally flat to gently rolling terrain, many of the easier and moderate routes are suitable for families. Look for routes that stick to the canal paths or the flatter sections around the Utxesa Reservoir for a pleasant family outing.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or attractions I can visit along the routes?
A: Along your rides, you can encounter several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2805726" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Utxesa Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; itself is a prominent feature. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4457173" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of Carrassumada&lt;/a&gt; or the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4508940" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de los Pueblos – One Tree for Each Town&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find parking for touring cycling routes in Aitona?
A: Aitona, as a starting point for many routes, typically offers parking options within the town. For specific routes, it's advisable to check the individual tour details on komoot, as many routes starting from villages or popular attractions will indicate suitable parking areas nearby.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Aitona?
A: Aitona experiences a Mediterranean climate, making spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) ideal for cycling. During these seasons, temperatures are mild, and the landscape is particularly vibrant. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cool, so plan accordingly.

Q: Are there any advanced touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 41 difficult routes around Aitona. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38154952" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segre River – Old Bridge of Seròs loop from Seròs&lt;/a&gt;, a 44.4-mile (71.4 km) route with significant elevation changes.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Aitona?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 20 reviews. Reviewers often praise the peaceful agricultural landscapes, the well-maintained canal paths, and the scenic beauty of the Utxesa Reservoir.

Q: Are there any routes that combine the Utxesa Reservoir and the Canal de Seròs Sluice Gates?
A: Yes, a popular route that combines both features is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31492074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canal de Seròs Sluice Gates – Utxesa Reservoir loop from Aitona&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 23.0-mile (37.0 km) path allows you to experience both the reservoir and the historic sluice gates.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to rest along the cycling routes?
A: While many routes traverse rural agricultural areas, routes that pass through or near towns like Aitona or Seròs will offer opportunities for cafes, restaurants, and places to restock. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer rural sections.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly touring cycling routes in Aitona?
A: Absolutely. Aitona offers 8 easy touring cycling routes, perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. These routes typically follow flatter sections along the canals or around the reservoir, providing a gentle introduction to the region's cycling opportunities.
tips:
We are in the Utxesa reservoir, an idyllic place to contemplate the flora and fauna of the area from the viewpoints/observatories that will be indicated to us.
It has an information booth where there are toilets, maps and in front of the booth, a picnic area to eat and rest.

The Ermita de Carrassumada is located on a small hill about 3 km from the center of the town of Torres de Segre. It is located in the south-eastern part of the municipality and is owned by the Diocese of Lleida.

Because of its elevated location, from its summit you can observe very good views of most of the Segrià region. At the foot of the tossal there are countless fruit orchards, the result of the transformation of a large expanse of barren land into irrigation. The strip of the river, contoured with green and above, to the west, the tabular mountain ranges of Aragon. And to the west, rises the conical rock of Montmeneu, the lonely and proud peak that is the highest peak of Segrià.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Utxesa Reservoir from Aitona — Circular through Segrià route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a varied terrain, primarily consisting of paved and asphalt roads (approximately 19.4 km combined). You'll also encounter about 10.6 km of paths, sections of dirt road, and parts of an old surveillance pathway. Be aware that some segments may be shared with motor vehicles.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for this touring bicycle route is around 2 hours and 6 minutes, covering a distance of nearly 30 kilometers.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll cycle around the beautiful Utxesa Reservoir, passing through wetlands and extensive fruit tree plantations. Keep an eye out for the Hermitage of Our Lady of Carrassumada. The route also offers panoramic views of the Secà reservoir from viewpoints like Joquers, and you might spot the Aitona castle and the "La Canadenca" hydroelectric power station.

faqs.question: Is the Utxesa Reservoir from Aitona — Circular through Segrià route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While Komoot classifies it as a "moderate" ride requiring "good fitness," other descriptions suggest it can be a "simple route for a low level." It's generally accessible, but some sections might require a moderate level of fitness or cycling experience.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The wetlands around the Utxesa Reservoir are a haven for birdlife. You can spot a variety of migratory and resident birds from designated observatories, including grey herons, purple herons, great crested grebes, cattle egrets, mallards, and black-winged stilts.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Aitona. While specific parking details aren't provided, Aitona is a common starting point, suggesting parking facilities are available within the town or near the reservoir access points.

faqs.question: Is the route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding dogs on this specific cycling route is not explicitly provided. Given it's a touring bicycle route that includes shared roads and natural areas, dogs are generally permitted but should be kept on a leash, especially near wildlife observatories and shared pathways.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are mentioned for cycling the Utxesa Reservoir from Aitona — Circular through Segrià route. It is an established Nature Trail, generally open to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The route is enjoyable for much of the year. Spring is particularly scenic due to the blooming peach trees in Aitona. The presence of migratory birds suggests spring and autumn could be excellent for birdwatching. Always check local weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants near the route?
faqs.answer: The route starts and ends in Aitona, where you can find local amenities including cafes and restaurants. There might also be options in other villages or towns you pass through in the Segrià region.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and length, it's advisable to bring sufficient water, snacks, a basic bike repair kit, and sun protection. Binoculars would be great for birdwatching, and a camera to capture the scenic views.
